question: Return a table showing repeat-customer performance by acquisition_channel, with columns acquisition_channel, ordering_customers, repeat_customers, and repeat_customer_rate_pct.
sql_query:
WITH customer_orders AS (
    SELECT
        c.customer_id,
        c.acquisition_channel,
        COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS order_count
    FROM core.customers AS c
    JOIN core.orders AS o ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id
    GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.acquisition_channel
)
SELECT
    acquisition_channel,
    COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) AS ordering_customers,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN order_count >= 2 THEN customer_id END) AS repeat_customers,
    ROUND(
        COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN order_count >= 2 THEN customer_id END)::numeric
        / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id), 0) * 100,
        2
    ) AS repeat_customer_rate_pct
FROM customer_orders
GROUP BY acquisition_channel
ORDER BY acquisition_channel
pandas_sql_query: WITH customer_orders AS (SELECT c.acquisition_channel, o.customer_id, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM customers c JOIN orders o USING (customer_id) GROUP BY 1, 2) SELECT acquisition_channel, COUNT(*) AS ordering_customers, SUM(CASE WHEN order_count > 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS repeat_customers, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN order_count > 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 4) AS repeat_customer_rate_pct FROM customer_orders GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY repeat_customer_rate_pct DESC, acquisition_channel
